title: CBSR directed corridor edge (v0.10.0)
description: Schema for one directed edge in analysis/computed_corridors_directed.json. Thirteen fields are universal across all 132 edges and are REQUIRED; the remainder are optional enrichment. This file is dependency-free to check: tools/verify_corridors_directed.py ships a minimal executor that validates every edge against THIS file, so the verifier and the schema cannot disagree, and tools/schema_reference_crosscheck.py re-validates the same corpus with the independent `jsonschema` reference implementation when it is installed. v0.10.0 adds `class_basis` (REQUIRED: the three-axis evidence model -- which precedence step decided this edge's class, on whose signal, of what claim_class, resting on an instrument of what binding_status), tightens `as_of_timeline` events into a typed shape with the trigger typology (`scheduled` / `scheduled_with_cap` / `contingent` and a `date_kind`), and structures `origin_override`. Historical note: through v0.9.99 `claim_class` and `binding_status` were not even schema fields at this layer, so the lawyer-citable intersection was undefined, not merely empty (eighth review NF-5). They now exist, are required on every edge, and are gated (EV1).
$comment: additionalProperties stays TRUE deliberately. The register's enrichment layer is open by design (Citable by Construction, §7.2: the dimension set is domain-specific and grows). Setting it to false would reject future authored fields and convert a forward-compatible corpus into a brittle one. Every field the corpus USES is nevertheless declared below, so an undeclared key is now a signal that something new arrived -- surfaced by the verifier as lint m6, not by a schema failure.

schema.description: Two tags are in use. v3-directed-edge is current; v2-rich is the legacy tag still on the HK->BR seed edge. Both validate; migrating the seed edge to v3 is tracked as lint m1 by the verifier, not enforced here.

feasibility_class.description: Human-readable prose for the DIRECTED class. Should lead with the same category as `class_code`; the one authored edge whose prose leads with the UNDIRECTED pair category (HK->BR, prose 'Category I/II', class_code 'II') is surfaced as non-fatal lint m5.

class_code.description: The DIRECTED feasibility class. DERIVED, never authored: tools/class_rule.py:class_of applies the Corridor Atlas v0.2.5 destination-first precedence to data/signal_table.json. Precedence: destination prohibition > destination no-regime > origin drag > destination in-transition > operative gate type.

compatibility_category.description: The UNDIRECTED pair category from the §5.14 matrix. Must equal the linked pair's category (enforced cross-layer in the full build: DC2). Distinct from `class_code`: 'I/II' is a legal value here and never a legal `class_code`.

compatibility_pair.description: Foreign key into the undirected §5.14 matrix: the canonical alphabetical key of {origin,destination}, e.g. 'UK-US'. Recomputed by the verifier as DC4a.

archetypes.description: Corridor archetypes: RC regulatory-comparability, SC sovereignty-constrained, TC transitional, DC digital-financial.

interaction_sets.description: The Architecture paper's six constraint-interaction sets. Per-direction refinement; may differ from the undirected pair's sets.

infrastructure_overlap.description: Structured wholesale-settlement-substrate tag (v0.9.93; was free text through v0.9.92). `bloc` is the machine-consumable partition value, checked on ALL 132 edges by verifier IB1 (authored edges are no longer exempt); `note` is optional prose. Tier-2 operational enrichment, NOT a proposition of law.

bloc.description: Settlement-substrate partition for THIS directed edge. agora/mbridge/ensemble = both ends share that experiment; cross-bloc = ends on rival blocs (Agora vs mBridge); none = at least one end off the shared experiments.

note.description: Optional human-readable gloss. Never consumed by IB1: the enum in `bloc` is authoritative, so no prose-prefix heuristic can misclassify an edge (the failure mode that hid the US<->HK cross-bloc edges through v0.9.92).

origin_drag.description: True exactly on the 29 edges whose class was decided by origin drag (the origin has no exportable authorizable token IN LAW -- token_regime, a tier1_legal signal, not token_in_issue). False where the destination's gate decided the class first, including the four tokenless-origin edges into a prohibition (CN) or a pre-regime (KR) destination. Cross-checked against class_basis.rule by gate EV1.

origin_override.description: Atlas v0.2.5 s.3.3: 'an origin that imposes an export or egress restriction contributes an origin-override flag'. Present on exactly the 33 edges out of CN (active: mainland capital controls), SG (active: MAS DTSP statement) and KR (conditional_dormant: Korea FX -- dormant because no exportable won token exists for it to bite on). Through v0.9.99 the flag sat on ONE edge and no gate checked it; gate OO1 now does.

materialized_from.description: Provenance partition of the 132 edges: 9 authored + 106 computed_skeleton + 17 computed_transition (verifier DC3).

as_of_timeline.description: Present on exactly the 25 `T` edges and on no others (gate TL2). The trigger typology of Feasibility Over Time s.3 is now expressible: `status` distinguishes a gazetted commencement (`scheduled`, UK 2027-10-25) from an earlier-of outer cap (`scheduled_with_cap`, US <= 2027-01-18, may fall sooner) from an enacted instrument with no gazetted commencement (`contingent`, TW). Through v0.9.99 only two slots existed and the US had no timeline at all.

divergence.description: Declared computed-vs-authored / directed-vs-undirected difference (labelled-preview discipline: a disagreement is surfaced as a finding, never silently reconciled).

evidence_tier.description: Provenance strength, per the three-axis evidence model (Citable by Construction s.2.2). resolution_text = confirmed against the official text; mixed = core point confirmed, operational detail pending; firm_summary = practitioner-corroborated. From v0.10.0 the binding-status CAP of s.3.1/s.4.2 (a resolution_text record must be in_force_enacted) IS enforced -- but on data/signal_table.json, by gate SIG2, not on this field. On the edge itself `evidence_tier` remains a declared label for the 8 authored edges; the per-record citable-integrity and ledger gates run in the FULL register's build, which is not shipped here (finding C1).

valid_as_of.description: ISO date or year-month. Both granularities occur in the corpus (8 edges at month precision, 1 at day precision).

authored_source_file.description: The root corridor YAML this authored edge was materialized from.

verification_backlog.description: Structured honest-residual backlog for THIS edge (v0.9.93). An open verification question lives here, not inline in an authoritative prose field: this is where the HK->BR seed edge's former inline 'CONFIRM date against SFC source' marker (lint m4) was re-homed.

field.description: The edge field the open question is about.

class_basis.description: WHY this edge has the class it has, as data rather than as prose. `rule` names the precedence step that fired; `governing_jurisdiction` is the origin for origin_drag and the destination otherwise; `signal` is the one signal read; `claim_class` MUST be tier1_legal (the signal-provenance gate of Citable by Construction s.4.2 -- a derived legal conclusion may never rest on a market fact); `binding_status` is the binding state of the instrument the class rests on; `record_ref` is the id of the node record carrying that instrument, and is now POPULATED (register v0.10.0 closes finding C1 / backlog VB-11 by shipping the 152 node records). It stays a bare locator string: the metadata that qualifies it lives in `record_ref_meta`, so a citation is never diluted by the caveat attached to it. Re-checked in full by verifier gate EV1.

signal.description: The only two signals class_of reads. `token_in_issue` (tier2_operational) is never read -- NF-3.

signal_value.description: The value of the one signal that fired. For an inbound_gate: authorization|channel|comparability|prohibited|none. For a token_regime: authorizable|not_commenced|prohibited|none. EV1 checks that the value implies the class -- e.g. a `destination_gate` edge on an `authorization` gate must be Category I, on a `channel` or `comparability` gate Category II.

claim_class.description: The kind of claim the class-driving signal makes. BOTH values are representable here on purpose: a schema that could not express the violation could not be used to detect it. The DISCIPLINE -- that a derived legal conclusion may rest only on a proposition of law, never on a market fact -- is enforced by the build gate EV1, not by this enum. That division is the argument of Citable by Construction s.4.1: 'a subset defined only by self-reported tags is a promise, not a guarantee'; the schema records the shape, the gate enforces the rule, and a negative test proves the gate bites.

binding_status.description: Binding state of the INSTRUMENT the class rests on -- distinct from the signal's legal EFFECT. Mainland China's prohibition instrument (银发〔2026〕42号) is `in_force_enacted` even though its effect is `prohibited`; conflating the two is what makes a naive check reject a correct edge. The effect lives in `signal_value`.

record_ref.description: The id of the node record carrying the instrument this class rests on (e.g. `us-pss-monetary_sovereignty-001`), or null where no record carries it and a locator would be a fabrication (citation firewall).

record_ref_meta.description: Qualifies the locator without diluting it. `match: same_instrument` means the record rests on the very instrument the class rests on; `related_dimension` means the record covers the same dimension under a DIFFERENT instrument (US and UK token_regime -- backlog VB-09). `divergence` is required wherever a same_instrument record's binding_status differs from the signal's (the load-bearing case is US s.18: Act-enacted vs section-operative -- errata E1).

record_ref_gap.description: Why no locator is offered, where record_ref is null. Never absent-and-silent.

jurisdiction.description: The twelve jurisdictions of the register. Tokenless origins (no exportable authorizable token): CN, TW, KR.

trigger_event.description: One change in law that would move this edge's class. Per Feasibility Over Time s.2.1 the event is always a tier1_legal commencement, enactment, or instrument taking effect -- never a market launch, a registration, or a licence cohort.

date_kind.description: gazetted = a fixed commencement day. outer_cap = an earlier-of upper bound that may resolve sooner (GENIUS s.20). ungazetted = enacted, commencement date not yet set.
